prerequisites: A Twilio account with access to the Twilio Console.
The Account SID and Auth Token from the Twilio Console Account Dashboard (the account_sid and auth_token secret fields).
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to read records (required).

authMethods.name: Account SID and Auth Token (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Twilio's REST API uses HTTP Basic authentication. Provide your Account SID as the username (account_sid) and your Auth Token as the password (auth_token). Both are found in the Twilio Console under the Account Dashboard. Twilio notes these are your primary account credentials with full access, so guard them carefully.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Twilio credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Twilio Console and open the Account Dashboard. Copy your Account SID and Auth Token from there. These are the two credentials the Twilio REST API uses for HTTP Basic authentication.

setupSteps.title: Provide the required credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter your Account SID into the account_sid field and your Auth Token into the auth_token field. Both are required and are stored as secrets.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which records should be read. Data created before this point is not synced.

setupSteps.title: Tune optional sync settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally adjust lookback_window (to re-read recent records and capture late-arriving updates), slice_step_duration (the time window size used when stepping through historical data), and num_workers (the number of concurrent workers). Leave these unset to use defaults.

sources.title: Making requests to the Twilio API (HTTP Basic authentication)
